minutes_briefs.title: BC privacy law removes health organizations from public body list
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has updated the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act by removing several health-related organizations from Schedules 2 and 3. Schedule 2 lists public bodies that must follow the Act's privacy rules. Schedule 3 lists health professional colleges that are also covered. The removed organizations include BC Clinical and Support Services Society, BC Patient Safety & Quality Council, BC Centre for Disease Control and Prevention Society, BC Health Services Purchasing Organization, BC Mental Health Society, BC Transplant Society, and several health colleges (Chiropractors, Dietitians, Massage Therapists, Naturopathic Physicians, Occupational Therapists). This means these organizations are no longer treated as public bodies under the Act, which changes how they handle personal information and respond to access requests.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If your business shares personal information with any of the removed organizations, review your privacy agreements to ensure they still comply with BC's privacy laws.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 78/2026
Made under: Freedom Of Information And Protection Of Privacy Act
Published in: No. 9 – B.C. Regs. 77 to 82, May 19, 2026
Deposit date: May 8, 2026

B.C. Reg. 78/2026, deposited May 8, 2026, under theFREEDOM OF INFORMATION AND PROTECTION OF PRIVACY ACT[section 76.1]. Ministerial Order M202/2026, dated May 7, 2026. I, Diana Gibson, Minister of Citizens’ Services, order that Schedules 2 and 3 to theFreedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act, R.S.B.C. 1996, c. 165, are amended as set out in the attached Appendix. — D. GIBSON,Minister of Citizens’ Services. 1	Schedule 2 to the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act, R.S.B.C. 1996, c. 165, is amended (a)	by striking out the following: BC Clinical and Support Services Society Chair of the BC Clinical and Support Services Society BC Patient Safety & Quality Council British Columbia Centre for Disease Control and Prevention Society Branch British Columbia Health Services Purchasing Organization British Columbia Mental Health Society Branch British Columbia Transplant Society Branch Health Care Practitioner’s Special Committee for Audit Hearings (Medicare Protection Act) Health Care Practitioners Special Committee for Audit Hearings (Medicare Protection Act) (a)	by striking out the following: College of Chiropractors of British Columbia College of Dietitians of British Columbia College of Massage Therapists of British Columbia College of Naturopathic Physicians of British Columbia College of Occupational Therapists of British Columbia
